title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Object-Lifetime Lens: Partial Initialization State
summary: A configuration loader acquires buffers, files, locks, and parsed objects before failures at each stage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem partial initialization state increment in failure-atomic resource constructor. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Review public APIs with an ownership table covering inputs, outputs, globals, nested allocations, and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Partial Initialization State · Theory Partial Initialization State
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9876. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
